name: Kreuzberg
tagline: Polyglot document intelligence: extract text, metadata, and code from 96 file formats
seoTitle: Kreuzberg: Fast Document Text Extraction Toolkit
metaDescription: Kreuzberg is a Rust-core document intelligence toolkit that extracts text, metadata, and code from 96 file formats with OCR, no GPU needed. Open source.
overview: Kreuzberg is an open-source document intelligence framework built on a Rust core. It reads 96 file formats, including PDFs, Office documents, images, HTML, emails, and archives, then returns clean text, metadata, and tables ready for search, RAG pipelines, or large language models.

Because the heavy lifting runs in Rust with SIMD optimizations and parallel processing, extraction is fast and does not need a GPU. Kreuzberg ships native bindings for many languages, so you can call the same engine from Python, Node.js, Rust, Go, Java, Ruby, and more, or run it as a CLI, REST API, or MCP server.

Beyond plain text, it can extract code intelligence from 306 programming languages via tree-sitter, pulling out functions, classes, imports, and docstrings, and it supports OCR backends such as Tesseract and PaddleOCR plus vision-model OCR for scanned files.
features: Reads 96 file formats across documents, spreadsheets, presentations, images, web, email, archive, and academic categories
Rust core with SIMD and full parallelism delivers fast extraction without requiring a GPU
OCR support via Tesseract (including a browser WASM build), PaddleOCR, EasyOCR, and vision-model OCR providers
Code intelligence for 306 programming languages through tree-sitter, returning functions, classes, imports, and docstrings
Native bindings for 14+ languages plus library, CLI, REST API, and MCP server deployment modes
Token-efficient TOON wire format and GFM-quality Markdown output for LLM and RAG pipelines

gettingStarted.intro: Kreuzberg offers a precompiled binary for every supported platform, so installation is quick. Pick the binding that matches your stack, or pull the Docker image to run it as a service.

steps.heading: Install for Python
steps.body: Install the PyPI package to use Kreuzberg from Python with async and sync APIs and built-in OCR backends.

steps.heading: Install for Node.js
steps.body: The Node.js package uses native NAPI-RS bindings for the fastest performance in JavaScript and TypeScript projects.

steps.heading: Add to a Rust project
steps.body: Use the core crate directly in Rust for flexible feature flags and zero-copy APIs.

steps.heading: Run with Docker
steps.body: Pull the official image to run Kreuzberg as a REST API, CLI, or MCP server without installing a language binding.

useCases: Building a RAG pipeline that needs clean text and metadata from mixed document types such as PDFs, Office files, and emails
Running OCR on scanned PDFs and images to recover searchable text without setting up a GPU
Indexing source code repositories by extracting functions, classes, and imports across hundreds of programming languages
Exposing a shared extraction service to many apps through the REST API or MCP server, callable from several languages
